rounced at the Great Northern
offices here. Railroad men agree
that it is the worst catastrophe
that has ever visited a railroad on
this continent.


No definite information can be
had as to when the Great Northern
line will be opened from this side
Of the mountains. The men at
work are forced to blast their way
along, as the track is covered with
trees. rocks, earth, ice and snow.
1t may be days before the line to
Wellington is opened from this
side. Telegraphic communication
with Wellington direct is still
broken and all word from there
comes around by the way of Seat
tie.


The men engaged in digging out
the buried trains are in momentary
danger of their lives, as slides are
continually taking place.
